message:
security(node): use full-width SHA-256 output to preserve collision resistance

Both interface_hash and transparency_verifier were truncating SHA-256 output
to 16 hex chars (64 bits), weakening collision resistance from 2^128 to 2^32
(birthday bound). This is insufficient for security-critical hash comparisons
where an attacker can generate collisions offline.

interface_hash.rs:
- Remove 16-char truncation in compute_hash(): output full 64-char hex
- Update verify_hash() length check: 16 → 64
- Update doc comments: "first 16 hexadecimal characters" → "full-width"
- Update test: assert hash_hex.len() == 64

transparency_verifier.rs:
- hash_pair(): Replace u64::from_le_bytes truncation with format!("{:x}")
  for full SHA-256 digest output
- leaf_hash(): Same truncation removal
- Update doc: "SipHash" reference → "full-width SHA-256"
- All downstream Merkle tree operations now use full-width hashes, making
  inclusion/consistency proofs collision-resistant at the 128-bit level

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
